work.name: University of Adelaide — Polo Lab (ACE & SAiGENCI)
work.summary: Developing computational methods to study gene regulation across cell development using spatial, single-cell transcriptomics and epigenomics.
highlights: Lead bioinformatics method and tool development across spatial and single-cell genomics projects.
Co-investigator on competitive grants (BioPlatforms Australia SA Framework; MRFF METASPATIAL).
Mentor PhD, graduate and undergraduate students; review grants and manuscripts.

work.name: Walter and Eliza Hall Institute of Medical Research (WEHI)
work.summary: Independent bioinformatics research and software development for spatial transcriptomics and functional genomics.
highlights: Authored the standR Bioconductor package for GeoMx DSP spatial transcriptomics.
Published in peer-reviewed journals and presented at national and international meetings.
Applied for fellowships and research grants.

education.score: Dean's Commendation for Doctoral Thesis Excellence
courses: Thesis: Three-dimensional regulation — establishing novel linkages between non-coding genetic variation and target genes.
Thesis: Investigating computational analysis pipelines and genomic proximity interactions in T lymphocytes.
